title: Scuse Me While I Miss The Sky

jokes.text: He didn't say anything about SkinnerInAShredder.com. Let's make this the best year ever.
jokes.setup: Skinner lists banned websites about him
jokes.punchline: Bart finds loophole for violent website while cheerfully saying 'best year ever'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - finding violent loophole while maintaining innocent enthusiasm. Strong misdirection and dark humor.

jokes.text: Well, they're not good enough to wipe my lens.
jokes.setup: Mentions respected documentary filmmakers
jokes.punchline: Arrogant dismissal of acclaimed directors
jokes.explanation: Establishes Declan's pretentious character through arrogant comparison. Solid character work.

jokes.text: No. Because they pass the savings on to me, the consumer.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's bothered by mad cow beef
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted consumer logic ignoring health risks
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - prioritizing savings over safety in perfectly in-character way.

jokes.text: Look, I give people a meatlike burger and some kind of cola
jokes.setup: Defending his restaurant's quality
jokes.punchline: Admits products aren't even real meat or cola
jokes.explanation: Krusty's casual admission of serving fake food with corporate spin language.

jokes.text: Good as new. [stapling burgers together]
jokes.setup: Documentary shows unsanitary food practices
jokes.punchline: Krusty cheerfully stapling discarded burgers
jokes.explanation: Gross-out visual gag enhanced by Krusty's casual attitude. Effective cringe comedy.

jokes.text: What about us cool kids? Should we just chill out?
jokes.setup: Director wants authentic behavior from students
jokes.punchline: Milhouse thinks he's cool and uses outdated slang
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse delusion - thinking he's cool while being completely uncool.

jokes.text: You're doing great. Stay with the dink.
jokes.setup: Milhouse asks about being cool
jokes.punchline: Director immediately identifies him as a loser
jokes.explanation: Brutal documentary filmmaker honesty delivered with professional detachment.

jokes.text: I chuck 'em at nerds, girls I like- whatever.
jokes.setup: Describing his dirt-throwing strategy
jokes.punchline: Casual admission he throws dirt at girls he likes
jokes.explanation: Captures elementary school boy logic about expressing romantic interest through aggression.

jokes.text: And in a flash, Bart's glory has gone the way of England's masculinity.
jokes.setup: Bart gets hit with dirt and loses status
jokes.punchline: British narrator making self-deprecating national joke
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel between Bart's downfall and British stereotypes. Self-aware cultural commentary.

jokes.text: My sash says 'Ultraman.'
jokes.setup: Hall monitor talking about dangers of job
jokes.punchline: Ralph mentions Japanese superhero on his sash
jokes.explanation: Pure Ralph randomness - completely missing the point of the conversation.

jokes.text: I haven't seen such a natural pair since 'half-sandwich and soup of the day.'
jokes.setup: Commenting on Lisa and Declan getting along
jokes.punchline: Awkward restaurant menu comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Skinner metaphor - trying to sound sophisticated but landing on mundane lunch combo.

jokes.text: Remember, as far as he knows, we still teach math.
jokes.setup: Whispering to teacher about documentary
jokes.punchline: School has abandoned teaching math
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of education system decline delivered as casual aside.

jokes.text: Well, I am young, gifted and yellow.
jokes.setup: Describing herself to documentary filmmaker
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'young, gifted and black' but references her cartoon color
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor referencing both the song and Lisa's animated nature while showing her intelligence.

jokes.text: picking and nibbling until, one day, you're 38 and managing a Barnes & Noble.
jokes.setup: Calling Lisa a 'buffet-style intellectual'
jokes.punchline: Specific corporate retail job as intellectual dead-end
jokes.explanation: Specific career prediction adds sting to general criticism about dilettantism.

jokes.text: Pick a path and follow it, or you'll just grow up, slog your way through Mount Holyoke and squeeze out babies.
jokes.setup: Giving Lisa career advice
jokes.punchline: Brutally pessimistic view of women's education and motherhood
jokes.explanation: Harsh but in-character cynicism from documentary filmmaker. Dark humor about academic achievement.

jokes.text: Even that kid that wears diapers is more popular. And he ain't popular.
jokes.setup: Explaining how unpopular Bart has become
jokes.punchline: Even the least popular kid (diaper-wearer) ranks higher, and he's not popular
jokes.explanation: Good structure establishing the hierarchy. The double-negative construction adds emphasis to Bart's fall.

jokes.text: Like after the time I accidentally called the teacher Mom.
jokes.setup: Claiming he always bounces back from embarrassment
jokes.punchline: References universal childhood embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Relatable childhood experience but not particularly original or character-specific.

jokes.text: Even I beat you up that day, after you passed out.
jokes.setup: Discussing Bart's embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Milhouse admits to hitting unconscious Bart
jokes.explanation: Escalates the humiliation while showing Milhouse's opportunistic cowardice perfectly.

jokes.text: Eh, I snapped it off some jerk's car. I could go to prison for life, but whatever.
jokes.setup: Asked about his hood ornament necklace
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of theft with extreme legal consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson character beat - criminal casualness about serious consequences.

jokes.text: All that's left for me is to become the biggest drunk this town's ever seen.
jokes.setup: Feeling defeated about losing cool status
jokes.punchline: Jumps to extreme alcoholism as solution
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart over-reaction to minor setback. Dark humor about child alcoholism.

jokes.text: Talkin' won't get you there. [gulping sounds]
jokes.setup: Bart declares intention to become town drunk
jokes.punchline: Homer encourages him while drinking
jokes.explanation: Homer being supportive of son's drinking ambition is perfectly in-character dark comedy.

jokes.text: And what's that? - I'm gonna die alone!
jokes.setup: Claims keeping options open lets him do what he wants
jokes.punchline: What he wants is to die alone
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with dark twist. Homer's cheerful delivery of depressing goal.

jokes.text: I spent 30 years brushing the teeth of dead monsters.
jokes.setup: Warning Lisa against dinosaur expertise
jokes.punchline: Describes paleontology as dental hygienist for corpses
jokes.explanation: Creative reframing of paleontology as mundane custodial work. Vivid and memorable.

jokes.text: home of a familiar blue planet we call... Rigel 7- not too far from Earth.
jokes.setup: Describing familiar blue planet in Milky Way
jokes.punchline: It's alien planet Rigel 7, not Earth
jokes.explanation: Science fiction reference misdirection. Solid structure but requires genre knowledge.

jokes.text: Will it be you? Or you? Or... you? [pointing at everyone]
jokes.setup: Interactive exhibit asking who will discover space wonders
jokes.punchline: Mechanical repetition keeps going after Lisa leaves
jokes.explanation: Simple mechanical malfunction gag. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Boi-oi-oi-oingggg!
jokes.setup: Check bounces at register
jokes.punchline: Homer makes sound effect of bouncing check
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish sound effect for financial failure is perfectly in-character.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Space monsters are invading us! - Dad, that's a moth.
jokes.setup: Homer panics about space invasion
jokes.punchline: It's just a moth attracted to telescope light
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to panic at mundane things. Lisa's deadpan correction works well.

jokes.text: Dad, that's a kaleidoscope. You may be a smart kid, Lisa, but you don't know much about not hurting people's feelings.
jokes.setup: Homer confused about telescope vs kaleidoscope
jokes.punchline: Criticizes Lisa's sensitivity while being ignorant
jokes.explanation: Homer deflecting his ignorance by attacking Lisa's emotional intelligence. Good character dynamic.

jokes.text: Thanks a lot, surviving Beach Boys.
jokes.setup: Concert music interferes with stargazing
jokes.punchline: Blames 'surviving' Beach Boys members
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about band member mortality while maintaining Lisa's intelligence and frustration.

jokes.text: For three seconds there, you were legally the mayor. - Yeah! And you was a girl, Joe!
jokes.setup: Moe explains temporary mayoral status during blackout
jokes.punchline: Barney adds incomprehensible observation about gender
jokes.explanation: Barney's drunken non-sequitur follows Moe's absurd legal claim. Character-driven nonsense.

jokes.text: No one ever wrote a poem about sickly orange barf glow.
jokes.setup: Lamenting light pollution ruining stars
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of artificial lighting as 'barf glow'
jokes.explanation: Poetic yet gross description perfectly captures Lisa's intellectual frustration with vivid imagery.

jokes.text: For astronomers like me- [mumbling] umb-hiven- this is a bigger problem, even than, oh, I don't know, say, getting a date, which is difficult for the geeky people.
jokes.setup: Explaining light pollution problem
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly admits dating difficulties for nerds
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink rambling social awkwardness. In-character but not exceptional.

jokes.text: They're like hermit crabs. They really are crabs- Oh, there's one now. I see you!
jokes.setup: Comparing astronomers to hermit crabs
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted spotting actual astronomer rival
jokes.explanation: Frink's paranoid academic competition plays out through animal metaphor.

jokes.text: That's not a hood ornament. It's a pacifier you spray-painted silver.
jokes.setup: Bart shows off his 'stolen' hood ornament
jokes.punchline: It's revealed as painted baby pacifier
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Bart's criminal pretensions. Effective humiliation comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, look. It's jill-of-all-trades. So, what's the ambition du jour?
jokes.setup: Nelson encountering Lisa with petition
jokes.punchline: Mocks her changing interests with pretentious French
jokes.explanation: Nelson's surprising vocabulary used to mock Lisa's intellectual pursuits. Good character development.

jokes.text: How'd you come up with that- tilt your head up?
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions bringing back night sky
jokes.punchline: Nelson reduces discovery to simple head movement
jokes.explanation: Nelson's crude reductionism contrasts with Lisa's idealism. Sharp economy of language.

jokes.text: Does it make you feel superior to tear down people's dreams? - Yes. Does it make you feel smart to question people's motives? - Yes. - Well, all right then.
jokes.setup: Lisa challenges Nelson's negativity
jokes.punchline: Both admit to their psychological motivations and accept it
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - both kids honestly confronting their psychology and reaching mature détente.

jokes.text: Yarr! I'd be happy to scrimshaw your petition.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks for signature on petition
jokes.punchline: Sea Captain offers to carve it in whale bone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sea Captain response using nautical terminology inappropriately. Strong character consistency.

jokes.text: or a faded Southern belle who needs the forgiving cloak of night to seduce naive young delivery boys with more pizza than common sense
jokes.setup: Mayor listing constituents who want darkness
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific scenario about aging seductress
jokes.explanation: Quimby's typically inappropriate oversharing disguised as political rhetoric.

jokes.text: Look out, Matthew Modine and Charlene Tilton. There are new stars in town- sky stars!
jokes.setup: Reporting on return of visible stars
jokes.punchline: Pun on celebrity stars vs astronomical stars
jokes.explanation: Standard news anchor wordplay. Competent but predictable pun structure.

jokes.text: cave-in to the astronomer lobby
jokes.setup: Describing city's decision on lighting
jokes.punchline: Implies astronomers have powerful political lobby
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of political influence through absurd special interest group.

jokes.text: Naturally, we blamed it on the Irish. We hanged more than a few.
jokes.setup: Describing past meteor shower as sky fire
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of ethnic scapegoating and lynching
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor delivered with Abe's casual racism. Effective but disturbing character moment.

jokes.text: Named after its discoverer- Professor Artemis Deadly- who was, ironically, killed in the shower of 1853.
jokes.setup: Explaining the 'Deadly Meteor Shower'
jokes.punchline: Scientist named 'Deadly' was killed by the phenomenon he discovered
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor with scientific naming convention joke. Well-structured.

jokes.text: This is great! It's darker than a French chick's armpit!
jokes.setup: Celebrating return of darkness
jokes.punchline: Crude comparison to French hygiene stereotype
jokes.explanation: Relies on ethnic stereotype for humor. In character for Carl but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: unmatched since the Detroit Tigers last made the play-offs over two centuries ago
jokes.setup: Describing current vandalism spree
jokes.punchline: Absurd timeframe for Detroit Tigers playoff drought
jokes.explanation: Sports futility joke with impossible timeline. Good structure but familiar territory.

jokes.text: The government has issued an orange alert, which, once again, means nothing.
jokes.setup: Reporting government response to vandalism
jokes.punchline: Alert system is meaningless bureaucracy
jokes.explanation: Good satire of post-9/11 alert systems. Timely political commentary.

jokes.text: Try-a the cheese-a pizza. It's greasy like-a you.
jokes.setup: Bart disguised as Italian valet
jokes.punchline: Insults Fat Tony while maintaining fake accent
jokes.explanation: Bart's risky insult of dangerous mobster while in disguise. Good tension building.

jokes.text: You're gonna depreciate a Mafia don's car? Hey, we're all gonna be murdered someday.
jokes.setup: Real Luigi warns about damaging Fat Tony's car
jokes.punchline: Bart's fatalistic acceptance of mob violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about inevitable death delivered with child-like acceptance.

jokes.text: But at the risk of enraging you by making you look-a stupid, we have no valet parking.
jokes.setup: Luigi needs to correct Fat Tony about valet service
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the danger of embarrassing a mobster
jokes.explanation: Good tension between truth-telling and self-preservation. Luigi's fear is palpable.

jokes.text: You mean that 10-year-old with a mustache was a phony?
jokes.setup: Learns there was no valet parking
jokes.punchline: Realizes obviously fake child valet was suspicious
jokes.explanation: Fat Tony's delayed realization about obviously fake scenario. Good comedic timing.

jokes.text: Let's fire blindly into the dark. - No. You might hit a made man.
jokes.setup: Police can't see in darkness
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's only concern is accidentally shooting mobster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum priorities - more worried about mob politics than public safety.

jokes.text: Hey. You're not John Ritter. - And you ain't that gorilla from the zoo. - Meh. Eh.
jokes.setup: Two characters mistake each other in darkness
jokes.punchline: Each thought the other was someone/something completely different
jokes.explanation: Mutual mistaken identity with absurd expectations. Both responses perfectly in character.

jokes.text: No one's ever won Moe's 'drink a gallon of gin' challenge. And no one ever will.
jokes.setup: Moe mentions drinking challenge at his bar
jokes.punchline: Implies the challenge is fatal
jokes.explanation: Moe's casual attitude toward potentially deadly drinking contest fits his character.

jokes.text: You know what this reminds me of- my Icelandic boyhood.
jokes.setup: Commenting on bright nighttime lights
jokes.punchline: References his ever-changing backstory
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Willie's constantly changing nationality and background.

jokes.text: What am I- superdad?
jokes.setup: Asked if he knows Lisa's position on lighting issue
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks basic parental awareness is superhuman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness about his low parenting standards. Great economy.

jokes.text: I'll bet somewhere there's a horse drinking coffee.
jokes.setup: Discussing how light has disrupted animal behavior
jokes.punchline: Imagines specific absurd animal confusion
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd image of animal disorientation. Creative and memorable.

jokes.text: Don't you think you're overreacting, talking gum-ball machine?
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing sleep deprivation effects
jokes.punchline: Homer hallucinates Lisa as candy dispenser
jokes.explanation: Sleep deprivation hallucination perfectly captures Homer's candy obsession and current state.

jokes.text: Don't you think you're overreacting, talking gum-ball machine?
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to talk to sleep-deprived Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer hallucinates Lisa as a gumball machine
jokes.explanation: Good sleep deprivation humor with specific, colorful hallucination.

jokes.text: you can't see anything in the sky except the Fox satellite
jokes.setup: Complaining about light pollution ruining sky viewing
jokes.punchline: Only visible celestial object is network's own satellite
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about the show's network dominating even fictional sky. Self-referential humor.

jokes.text: You can say it. I add nothing.
jokes.setup: Lisa struggling to describe Bart's contribution
jokes.punchline: Bart honestly admits his uselessness
jokes.explanation: Bart's self-awareness about his limitations. Refreshingly honest sibling dynamic.

jokes.text: Aw, my baby's first words.
jokes.setup: Security system recognizes Maggie's palm print
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks electronic voice is Maggie talking
jokes.explanation: Homer's misunderstanding of technology as parental milestone. Typical confused response.

jokes.text: Gandhi also said, 'Less talk, more rock!'
jokes.setup: Lisa references Gandhi's philosophy on civil disobedience
jokes.punchline: Bart attributes rock concert slogan to Gandhi
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart move - ending philosophical discussion with fake Gandhi quote that sounds like concert promotion.

jokes.text: But, Chief, we are the entire force.
jokes.setup: Chief wants entire police force working on blackout
jokes.punchline: Springfield police department is only two people
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline about Springfield's minimal public services. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'll catch 'em on my tongue for luck. Ow! Ow! Ow!
jokes.setup: Willie notices falling glass shards
jokes.punchline: Tries to catch sharp glass with his tongue
jokes.explanation: Willie's misguided folk wisdom leads to predictable injury. Character-appropriate but obvious.

jokes.text: I can't read porno by candlelight! Who am I- Abe Lincoln!
jokes.setup: Angry mob complaining about power outage effects
jokes.punchline: Compares porn reading to Lincoln's log cabin studying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of Comic Book Guy's habits with Lincoln mythology. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: I wish Jimmy the Snitch could see this. [muffled voice] Well, just open the trunk!
jokes.setup: Fat Tony expressing wish that Jimmy could see beautiful stars
jokes.punchline: Jimmy is alive but trapped in car trunk
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy setup - seeming sentimentality immediately undercut by mob violence revelation.

jokes.text: I wish God were alive to see this.
jokes.setup: Everyone appreciating beautiful night sky
jokes.punchline: Bart implies God is dead while trying to be reverent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart theological confusion - attempts at spirituality result in accidental blasphemy. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: I wish God were alive to see this.
jokes.setup: Family enjoying beautiful starry sky
jokes.punchline: Homer expresses wish that God were alive to see creation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer theological confusion - touching sentiment undermined by fundamental misunderstanding of God's nature.

jokes.text: I'm gonna live with Underground Grandma.
jokes.setup: Asked where he'll be in seven years
jokes.punchline: References mysterious underground grandmother
jokes.explanation: Pure Ralph weirdness - creates entire mythology in four words. Perfectly captures his unique worldview.

jokes.text: Soccer mom! - I'll be batting .352 in the majors!
jokes.setup: Kids sharing career predictions
jokes.punchline: One kid insults another as 'soccer mom' while third makes specific baseball claim
jokes.explanation: Childhood dreams and playground insults. Functional but not particularly memorable.

tmdb_overview: A snobby British filmmaker named Declan Desmond begins making a documentary about student life at Springfield Elementary School. When introduced to Lisa, he tells her that she has too many goals and suggests that she pick one career. Lisa focuses on astronomy, but cannot watch the stars because of light pollution. She convinces Mayor Quimby to decrease the light's intensity during the night, which only results in an increase in the crime rate. Quimby bows to public pressure and switches the lights back on permanently. Lisa overloads the generators at the Power Plant, causing a power outage, and is able to watch a meteor shower undisturbed by light.
